notes: Tikhonov regularization (= ridge regression / L2 penalty; also ill-posed inverse problems) is named after Russian mathematician Andrey Tikhonov. Russian –Ę–łŐĀ—Ö–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ = [ňąt ≤ix…ôn…ôf] (per Wiktionary): stress on the FIRST syllable ‚ÄĒ TEE-khuh-nawf (the reduced final vowel is closer to 'nuf') ‚ÄĒ with a guttural 'kh' (/x/, a single throaty sound, not 'k-h') and a final 'v' that devoices toward 'f'. English speakers almost always shift stress to the second syllable, tik-HOH-nov, and say a plain 'k' + 'h'. The name also surfaces transliterated as 'Tychonoff' (Tychonoff's theorem in topology), reinforcing that the 'kh' is one guttural sound.
